title: Structured Output, Pydantic, JSON Schema, Validation, and Repair · Behavior Case: Json Schema Boundary
summary: The model returns JSON-looking text that is coerced into domain objects, blocked outputs are parsed as success, schema drift is hidden, and invalid values are silently repaired. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the json schema boundary increment in strict versioned agent output boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.
Validate provider output with strict Pydantic models, explicit bounds, discriminated variants, forbidden extras, and actionable errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: agentpy-json-schema-boundary — Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-structured-output-validation-5
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-structured-output-validation-5.
# Case 9036: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it.
def worked_agentpy_structured_output_validation_5(candidate_outputs: int = 24, valid_outputs: int = 15, rejected_outputs: int = 9, schema_versions: int = 3, unsafe_coercions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorityworked5",
        "interaction9036",
        "tool5dekrow",
        "stateworked59036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if candidate_outputs < 1 or valid_outputs < 0 or rejected_outputs < 0:
        return False, candidate_outputs, "output-count"
    if valid_outputs + rejected_outputs != candidate_outputs:
        return False, valid_outputs, "output-reconciliation"
    if schema_versions < 2:
        return False, schema_versions, "schema-version"
    if unsafe_coercions > 0:
        return False, unsafe_coercions, "unsafe-coercion"
    assert valid_outputs + rejected_outputs == candidate_outputs and unsafe_coercions == 0
    return True, valid_outputs, "strict-structured-output"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Json Schema Boundary model · Theory Json Schema Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Json Schema Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory agent engineering team handles case 7179 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate structured final output with versioned Pydantic and JSON Schema contracts, explicit rejection, and bounded repair; loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Json Schema Boundary model · Theory Json Schema Boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.
